decks.name: Lines and Angles
decks.description: Learn about different types of lines and angles around you

cards.back: A straight path that connects two points. It has a starting point and an ending point.
cards.hint: Think of the edge of your ruler

cards.back: A straight path that starts at one point and goes on forever in one direction.
cards.hint: Think of a flashlight beam - it starts at the torch and keeps going

cards.back: An angle that measures exactly 90 degrees. It looks like the corner of a book or a door.
cards.hint: Look at the corner of your notebook

cards.back: Two lines that run side by side and never meet, no matter how far you extend them.
Two lines that meet at a right angle (90 degrees), forming an L shape or a plus (+) shape.
cards.hint: Think of the corner where two walls meet

decks.name: Triangles
decks.description: Explore the different types of triangles and their special features

cards.front: How many sides and angles does a triangle have?
cards.back: A triangle always has 3 sides and 3 angles.
A triangle where all 3 sides are equal in length and all 3 angles are equal (60 degrees each).
A triangle that has exactly 2 sides of equal length. The two angles opposite those sides are also equal.
cards.hint: Think of a slice of pizza - two edges are the same length

cards.back: A triangle where all 3 sides are different lengths and all 3 angles are different.

cards.front: What do all the angles of a triangle add up to?
cards.back: The three angles of any triangle always add up to 180 degrees.
cards.hint: It is the same as two right angles put together

decks.name: Quadrilaterals
decks.description: Discover four-sided shapes and what makes each one special

cards.back: Any shape that has exactly 4 sides and 4 angles. 'Quad' means four.
cards.hint: Look around your classroom - many objects have four sides

cards.front: What is special about a rectangle?
cards.back: A rectangle has 4 sides with 4 right angles (90 degrees each). Opposite sides are equal and parallel.
cards.hint: Think of a door or a book cover

cards.front: What is special about a square?
cards.back: A square has 4 equal sides and 4 right angles. It is a special type of rectangle.
cards.hint: Think of a chessboard tile - all sides are the same

cards.back: A four-sided shape where opposite sides are equal in length and parallel to each other.
cards.hint: Imagine pushing a rectangle sideways so it leans over

cards.back: A four-sided shape where all 4 sides are equal in length. It looks like a tilted square.
cards.hint: Think of a diamond shape on a playing card
